meta.title: Kamui Ski Links
meta.description: About 30 minutes from Sapporo—urban-edge powder and 4,000 m groomed runs. The go-to day-trip hill.

hero.title:
Kamui
Ski Links
hero.tagline: Urban-edge powder and long groomers—the Sapporo day-trip default.

snow.label: Snow quality
snow.note: Inland cold over Ishikari plain · quieter zones

sanctuary.title:
Sapporo's backyard,
4,000 m groomers.
sanctuary.lead: A big hill on the Ishikari hills, ~30–40 minutes by car from central Sapporo. Morning powder and 4,000 m groomed runs in one day—a rare urban-edge setup. Real snow and 601 m vertical without staying at a mega resort or driving to Niseko.
sanctuary.noticeBody: Direct ski buses from Sapporo Station and Sapporo TV Tower, Hanakawa Station shuttles, and 4,000 parking spaces—shortest links between city and slopes. Check snow and roads on kamui-skilinks.com and live cameras before you go; easy day trips even the day after your flight.
sanctuary.imgAlt: Long groomers over the Ishikari plain—urban-edge powder runs

primary.title: 4,000 m max × 601 m vertical
primary.lead: Kamui Eye gondola to the summit—King Course and more, with ~30% beginner, 50% intermediate, 20% advanced. Powder stashes and long groomers in one day; rare scale this close to a major city.
primary.imgAlt: Ishikari plain from the gondola—long groomers and powder lanes

secondary.title: Sapporo day-trip flow
secondary.lead: ~30–40 minutes from central Sapporo; direct buses and Hanakawa shuttles mean no car required. Morning powder → afternoon groomers → Jozankei onsen → Sapporo ramen at night—high density without resort lodging.

bundle.title:
Day trip,
serious vertical.
bundle.lead: Adult day pass ¥5,300 (2025–26)—Niseko-grade snow and 4,000 m runs while staying in Sapporo. Season passes, multi-day tickets, and family deals cut repeat-visit cost; built for weekend commuters from the city.
bundle.imgAlt: Kamui Center House—on-hill rest and dining base

wellness.title:
Then Ishikari nabe
and ramen.
wellness.lead: Lunch at Kamui Center House on the hill, Ishikari nabe with local seafood in Ishikari city on the way back, miso ramen in Susukino or Jozankei onsen to recover—ski, food, and baths within 30–60 minutes.
wellness.leadStation: Ramen alley near Sapporo Station fits bus and JR connections on the way home.
wellness.imgAlt: Ishikari nabe and Sapporo miso ramen—corridor après-ski

tour.title:
Stay in Sapporo,
ski Kamui.
tour.lead: Hotel in Sapporo the night before. ~30 minutes by bus or car, morning powder and afternoon groomers. Jozankei onsen at dusk, Sapporo ramen at night—maximize experience density without resort lodging.

01.title: Sapporo city hotel
01.body: Near Sapporo Station or Odori—city food and early departure

02.title: Bus or car ~30 min
02.body: Direct ski bus from station & TV Tower · Hanakawa shuttles

03.title: Kamui Ski Links
03.body: ¥5,300 day pass · powder and 4,000 m groomers

04.title: Jozankei Onsen
04.body: Stop on the way back · open-air bath recovery

05.title: Sapporo ramen
05.body: Miso, shio, or shoyu—finish in Susukino

ecosystem.title: Sapporo corridor & local economy
ecosystem.body: Lodging, dining, and baths center on Sapporo and Ishikari town while the hill serves day trips—spending flows to Ishikari nabe, Sapporo ramen, and Jozankei without overtourism pressure. Season pass ¥66,000 supports weekend commuters balancing city life and real skiing.

access.title: Sapporo hub & last mile
access.body: ~30–40 minutes by car from central Sapporo. Seasonal direct buses from Sapporo Station and TV Tower, shuttles from JR Hanakawa on the Ishikari Line, 4,000 parking spaces—~70–90 minutes from New Chitose Airport by car with straightforward rental routes.

green.title: Ishikari food culture year-round
green.body: Winter brings Ishikari nabe and Kamui snow; summer brings salmon fishing on the Ishikari River and camping—the plain's terroir extends beyond ski season. Day-trip skiers become year-round regional fans.

access.note: Central Sapporo ~30–40 min by car · seasonal direct buses from station & TV Tower · JR Hanakawa shuttles · 4,000 parking spaces
access.phoneBus: Bus & shuttle times on official site
access.places: 北海道Hokkaido石狩市Ishikari花川北6条Hanakawa-Kita 6-jo1-40
access.rentacarEyebrow: 都市近郊のパウダー＆ロンググルーム
access.rentacarLink: 新千歳空港でレンタカー予約
access.rentacarNote: スカイチケット（外部サイト）
access.rentacarHint: カムイスキーリンクス周辺のドライブ向け

hero.title:
Four tastes
beyond slope food.
hero.tagline: Center House to Ishikari nabe and Sapporo ramen—corridor dining

nearbyFood.notice: Hours and menus are on each venue's official site.
nearbyFood.introTitle: Why food drives the day trip
nearbyFood.introLead: From on-hill lunch to rewards on the way home—real local food within 30–60 minutes is the urban-edge ski advantage.
nearbyFood.introBody: On the hill, après-ski, and Sapporo transit—four picks along your route.
nearbyFood.spotsLead: From the hill into Ishikari town and Sapporo, in route order.

hokurei.title: Kamui Center House
hokurei.lead: Slope-side rest and dining. Curry udon, ramen, rice bowls—warm skier fare, ideal after morning powder.

yuyu.title: Ishikari nabe specialty (Ishikari city)
yuyu.lead: Cod, salmon, scallops, and vegetables in miso broth—Hokkaido's signature winter pot. Stop in Ishikari city on the way back for the real thing.

pipicafe.title: Sapporo ramen (Susukino & station area)
pipicafe.lead: Finish with Sapporo miso ramen. Classic shops in Susukino and station-area favorites—easy to pair with bus or JR connections.

otokoyama.title: Ishikari River salmon & seafood
otokoyama.lead: Salmon from the Ishikari River and Sea of Japan catch—for travelers who want ski and Hokkaido seafood in one day. Local izakaya in Ishikari city.

hero.title:
Jozankei baths,
ease the legs.
hero.tagline: Default stop after Kamui—Sapporo's premier onsen village

nearbyOnsen.notice: Fees and hours are on each facility's official site.
nearbyOnsen.introTitle: Onsen at the core of the corridor
nearbyOnsen.introLead: ~40–50 minutes from the hill—Jozankei onsen village is the après anchor for Sapporo-stay plans.
nearbyOnsen.introBody: From classic ryokan to day-use baths—two bases that fit Sapporo lodging itineraries.
nearbyOnsen.spotsLead: Recovery after skiing and lodging bases.

yuyu.title: Jozankei Onsen (Hoheikyo etc.)
yuyu.lead: ~50 minutes from Sapporo, en route from Kamui. Open-air baths and sauna for sore legs—day visit or overnight.

kamoi.title: Natural onsen hotels near Sapporo Station
kamoi.lead: For Sapporo city stays—station-area hotels with natural baths. Urban après, then direct bus to Kamui next morning.

hero.title:
Stay in Sapporo,
commute to Kamui.
hero.tagline: City stay + day ski vs near-hill lodging

corridorStay.notice: Rooms, rates, and shuttles are on each property's booking site.
corridorStay.introLead: Sleep in Sapporo and bus commute, or stay near Ishikari for dawn powder—choose by what your trip is about.

yuyu.title: Hanakawa · Ishikari area lodging
yuyu.lead: ~10 minutes to the hill by car—first-track powder for core skiers. Near-hill hotels and ryokan with parking beat city commute time.

mount-city.title: Ishikari city hotels
mount-city.lead: Ishikari nabe after skiing, sleep nearby—good access to both hill and town dining in the Ishikari area.

omo7.title: Sapporo Station hotels (JR Tower · Odori)
omo7.lead: Flagship corridor stay—Sapporo ramen and Genghis Khan at night, direct bus to Kamui in the morning.

dormy.title: Natural onsen hotel near Sapporo Station
dormy.lead: Low friction near the station. Post-ski bath and morning bus—strong choice before the trip home.
